events.name: Bone Wind
events.gmPrompt: Somewhere deeper, the catacombs draw breath and exhale slow. A draft begins moving through the chamber, carrying fine bone dust on currents that have no source anyone can point to. The cold is sharper than the underground alone explains. The dead do not move here, but the place remembers them.
events.checkContext: Survival check to read the draft path and arrange the camp clear of the worst flow before the bone dust settles and the cold bites in.
events.gmGuidance: Residual magical effect, not a creature. At some hour of the night the catacombs exhale: a slow draft from deeper levels, carrying fine bone dust and a chill sharper than ambient underground temperature alone. The cause is the accumulated weight of so many dead in one place. The place stores cold and releases it. The draft lasts an hour or two, then settles. The threat is exposure (deep cold biting through warmth) and contamination (bone dust settling on open gear). The watcher's task is to read where the draft is coming from, which niche face it pours through, and arrange the camp out of the main flow. Stoking the fire helps once the camp is repositioned. escalate without a watch because the draft starts mid-night with nobody to read the chamber. Mold Earth or Stone Shape can seal the niche the draft pours from. Control Flames sustains the campfire against the cold. Endure Elements grants a caster full cold immunity. Avoid attributing the draft to a specific god, undead type, or magical school. The place remembers. That is all the explanation needed.
events.description: A slow draft from deeper in the catacombs. Cold sharper than it should be, carrying fine bone dust.

onTriumph.narrative: The draft caught the watcher's attention before it built. Camp shifted into a side bay where the flow could not reach, fire stoked under bare stone. The chamber breathed through the night and the camp did not feel it.

onSuccess.narrative: The draft path read clean enough to shift the gear and bedrolls out of the main flow. The cold passed through the chamber without finding warmth to bite.

onMixed.narrative: Half the camp was clear of the draft. The other half spent the worst hour of it in the flow. Bone dust through open ration packs, bedrolls clammy with cold. Sleep light and broken.

effects.description: Rations dusted by the bone wind.
Cold draft and bone dust disrupted sleep.

onFailure.narrative: The camp sat in the flow all night. By dawn the bedrolls are stiff with cold, the gear is coated in fine grey dust, and rations packs that were open are unusable. Everyone wakes shaking, slow to warm, slower to think.

effects.description: Rations contaminated by bone dust through the night.
A night spent in the cold draft. Sleep barely earned the name.
A bone-deep chill that lingers into the day.

events.name: Cobweb Fire
events.gmPrompt: Above the camp, the vaulted ceiling is a tangled mass of grey cobwebs: generations of accumulation, dense as cloth in places, dry as paper. The campfire's updraft is already drying it further. A spark on the wrong eddy would catch.
events.checkContext: Perception check to read the heat zone above the fire and clear the worst tangles before the cobweb mass ignites.
events.gmGuidance: The cobweb mass above the camp is dry, layered, and flammable. A campfire's updraft or a careless torch lights a slow crawl of flame across the ceiling. It will not consume the chamber, but it will scorch gear, foul the air with smoke, and force the camp out into the corridor for hours while the burn runs its course. The fix is simple: knock the worst tangles down with a pole before settling, or move the fire under a section of bare stone vault. The watcher's task is to identify the most flammable patches by torchlight before lighting up. escalate without a watch because the spark catches mid-night with nobody to smother it. Create Bonfire underneath a thick web mass is a bad idea and the player should be told so before they regret it. Prestidigitation can dampen a small patch. Druidcraft will reliably predict whether a given fire location will catch. Mold Earth into a stone canopy is overkill but it works.
events.description: The vaulted ceiling is a tangled mass of dry cobwebs. The campfire's updraft is drying it further. A spark on the wrong eddy would catch.

onTriumph.narrative: The worst tangles came down with a pole before the fire was lit. The vault above the camp ended in bare stone. The cobweb sea hung clear, well away from the heat. No catch, no smoke.

onSuccess.narrative: The fire moved under a section of bare vault before lighting up. The cobweb mass smouldered once at the edges and went out on its own. A close call, but a clean night.

onMixed.narrative: A patch of the ceiling caught and crawled. Smothered before it spread, but smoke filled the chamber for an hour and the air smelled of burnt silk all night. Sleep light, throats raw.

effects.description: Smoke and burnt silk fouled the air. Sleep light.

onFailure.narrative: Flame skipped along the cobweb ceiling and ran across the vault. Camp out into the corridor for hours, the chamber too hot and too smoky to sit in. By the time the burn died down, gear was scorched, ration packs near the fire had smoked through to inedible, and a waterskin propped against the wall split in the heat.

effects.description: Rations spoiled by heat and smoke.
Waterskin split in the heat near the fire.
Camp displaced for hours, smoke through everything.

events.name: Spider Brood
events.gmPrompt: A thread of silk drifts down across the firelight. Then another. Above the camp, in the dense cobweb mass woven through the broken vaults, something is moving. Pale legs working their way down. Half a dozen of them. They are following the body heat below.
events.checkContext: Nature check to read the descent lines from the cobweb mass and pull the camp clear of the worst section before the brood reaches the sleepers.
events.gmGuidance: Catacomb spiders. Bigger than a coin, smaller than a fist. Pale from generations in the dark. They live in the cobweb ceiling, suspended bodies waiting for warmth to signal prey. A small brood (four to six) is descending toward the camp. The threat is the bite (sharp, mildly venomous), not a fight. They drop onto a sleeper, strike, and retreat back into the web mass to digest. This is not a combat encounter. A spider that has bitten will not stay to engage, and the brood will not commit if the prey defends itself. Do not run initiative. The watcher's task is to read the silk lines from the ceiling (Nature identifies where descent paths cluster) and move the camp away from the worst section. auto_fail without a watch because nobody sees them coming and they reach the bedrolls undisturbed. Speak with Animals returns hunger and patience. The spiders will accept a deferred meal if persuasively asked, then return when the heat draws them back. Faerie Fire highlights descending bodies clearly. Daylight cantrip cast in the web mass at setup disrupts the colony's hunting and may prevent the event.
events.description: Pale spiders descend from the cobweb mass above the camp, following the body heat below.

onTriumph.narrative: The silk lines read clean before the camp settled. Three paces clear, beneath a section of open vault where the web mass thinned to nothing. The brood worked downward toward where the camp had been, found no warmth, and went back up the silk to wait for warmer prey.

onSuccess.narrative: The first descending body caught the torchlight in time. Raised flame, raised voice. The brood retreated up the silk and held there, watching but unwilling. They did not come back down before dawn.

onMixed.narrative: One reached a bedroll before the alarm went up. A bite to the shoulder, the spider scrabbling back up its silk before a hand could close on it. Painful, swollen by morning, and the camp tense for the rest of the night.

effects.description: Spider bite. Swelling persists into the day.
Camp on edge after the bite. Light, restless sleep.

onFailure.narrative: A descending body went unseen until it was on a sleeper. A bite, the spider retreating up the silk before a hand could close on it. The bitten wakes feverish and shaken, the venom slow to leave.

effects.description: Spider bite. Swelling into the day.
Spider venom. Persists through the next rest.

events.name: Sealed Alcove
events.gmPrompt: Off the main passage, half-concealed by fallen rubble, a smaller niche sealed at the front with old wax and a clay plug. The seal looks intact. Whatever was placed inside has had nothing but darkness and dry air for a very long time.
events.checkContext: Investigation check to read the seal and open the alcove without breaking the contents.
events.gmGuidance: Reward event. Watch-only. The alcove was sealed long ago and the seal has held against time. Contents survived because the air inside is dry and undisturbed. Triumph yields the best preserved items: a sealed oil lamp, a preserved healing draught, and several preserved rations. Success yields the lamp and some rations. Mixed and Failure yield nothing in line with the standard salvage pattern (most of the contents shattered on opening, or the seal failed years ago). The contents are not magical and not cursed. They are practical, preserved by sealing. Avoid campaign hooks. This is not a quest item, not a relic, not a clue. Just a small dry larder somebody put aside and never came back for.
events.description: A side niche sealed with old wax and a clay plug. The seal has held; the contents survived.

onTriumph.narrative: A clean read on the seal. The wax broke evenly, the clay plug lifted intact. Inside, dry as the day it was sealed: a small brass lamp still full of oil, a wrapped bundle of preserved rations, and a wax-sealed vial that hisses faintly when uncorked. A burial preparation never used.

description.value: A small brass oil lamp sealed with wax against the catacomb air. Still full, still functional. Provides hours of warm light.
Dried food sealed in clay against the catacomb air. Long preservation has kept it edible. Good for a meal.

onSuccess.narrative: The seal gave with a careful pry. Inside, a brass lamp still functional and a sealed bundle of preserved food. The clay jar at the back had cracked long ago; its contents lost. The rest survives.

description.value: A small brass oil lamp sealed with wax against the catacomb air. Still full, still functional. Provides hours of warm light.
Dried food sealed in clay against the catacomb air. Long preservation has kept it edible. Good for a meal.

onMixed.narrative: The seal broke uneven. The lamp's bowl was already cracked under the wax. The clay plug shattered and most of the contents scattered into dust as the niche opened. Nothing salvaged usable.

onFailure.narrative: The seal lifted easily. Too easily. It had failed years ago and only sat in place. Inside the niche is a layer of dust and the dry suggestion of what was once a bundle. Nothing worth the effort.

events.name: Burial Tribute
events.gmPrompt: Set into the wall above the gear pile, a small niche sealed by a flat stone slab. A single name carved deep into the slab face. One word, deeply cut. Where the slab meets the niche frame, pressed into the seam by careful fingers long ago, a handful of coins left as tribute.
events.checkContext: Investigation check to lift the coins free of the seam without disturbing the slab.
events.gmGuidance: Reward event. Watch-only. A burial niche sealed with a name slab; a small coin offering set into the seam beneath the name as tribute. Closed-loop fiction: a person died, was sealed in, somebody left coins to mark the moment. The story ends there. No faction, no destination, no quest pointer. If a player presses for more, the answer is that the inscription is old and there is nothing else to find. The coins are a small reward; the niche is atmosphere. Triumph and success yield coins. Mixed yields a single coin shaken loose. Failure means a coin falls into the seam and is gone; nothing recovered. The slab does not need to be removed and should not be. Do not open the niche.
events.description: A burial niche with a single carved name. A small coin offering pressed into the seam beneath.

onTriumph.narrative: Careful work along the seam. The coins came free one at a time, more than the count suggested at first glance. Whoever left this offering left generously, and pressed the deepest coins fully into the join.

description.value: Old coins left as a burial tribute in the catacombs. Tarnished by long darkness, but still spendable.

onSuccess.narrative: The visible coins came free with a slow hand. A handful, modest but real, dropped one at a time into the palm.

description.value: Old coins left as a burial tribute in the catacombs. Tarnished by long darkness, but still spendable.

onMixed.narrative: A single coin worked free of the seam. The rest are stuck deeper than the angle allows, and pulling harder would shift the slab. Best left.

description.value: Old coins left as a burial tribute in the catacombs. Tarnished by long darkness, but still spendable.

onFailure.narrative: First coin out, then the second slipped past the fingertip and dropped into the seam, gone behind the slab. The rest are no longer reachable. Lost to the seam.

events.name: Long Silence
events.gmPrompt: No wind. No insects. No drip. No echo. The catacombs hold their silence like something stored. Breathing carries further than it should. A boot scuff is loud enough to make heads turn. The dead make no objection, and the living find themselves matching the silence.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. No mechanical consequence. The catacombs are sealed deep enough that air does not move and sound does not bounce away. The silence is total. If the table wants it, a moment for characters to reflect on the weight of so many dead. If they do not, skip it and move on. Rest undisturbed. Do not narrate ghostly whispers or movements seen from the corner of the eye unless the campaign has actively introduced such a threat. Default to nothing happening.
events.description: Sealed silence. No wind, no insects, no drip, no echo. Breathing carries further than it should.
